seoTitle: MindMotion News | MCU Product Updates | LiTong
seoDescription: Latest news and updates from MindMotion including new product releases, technology announcements, and industry events. Stay informed with authorized distributor LiTong.

news.title: MindMotion Launches MM32F5270 Series High-Performance MCUs
news.summary: MindMotion introduces the MM32F5270 series featuring ARM Cortex-M4F cores up to 168MHz with DSP and floating-point capabilities, targeting high-performance industrial and consumer applications.
contentParagraphs: MindMotion Technology has officially launched the MM32F5270 series, a new family of high-performance microcontrollers based on the ARM Cortex-M4F core. The new series delivers exceptional processing power with frequencies up to 168MHz, making it ideal for demanding applications requiring complex algorithms and high-speed processing.

The MM32F5270 series features a comprehensive set of peripherals including USB 2.0 OTG, Ethernet MAC with IEEE 1588 support, dual CAN 2.0B interfaces, and advanced analog peripherals. The integrated hardware encryption engine provides AES, DES, and SHA acceleration for secure applications.

Key features of the MM32F5270 series include: ARM Cortex-M4F core with DSP instructions and single-precision FPU, up to 512KB Flash memory with dual-bank support for live firmware updates, up to 128KB SRAM, 12-bit ADC with up to 3Msps sampling rate, and comprehensive security features.

The new series is particularly well-suited for industrial automation, digital power control, advanced motor control, and IoT gateway applications. The pin-to-pin compatibility with existing MM32F3270 series enables easy upgrades for existing designs.
highlights: 168MHz Cortex-M4F with DSP and FPU
Up to 512KB Flash with dual-bank support
Ethernet MAC with IEEE 1588 precision time protocol
Hardware encryption engine for secure applications
Pin-to-pin compatible with MM32F3270 series
news.impact: The MM32F5270 series provides a cost-effective alternative to high-performance MCUs from international suppliers, enabling Chinese manufacturers to develop competitive industrial and consumer products with reduced BOM costs.

faqs.question: What is the main advantage of MM32F5270 over MM32F3270?
faqs.answer: The MM32F5270 series offers several advantages over the MM32F3270 series: (1) Higher performance - 168MHz Cortex-M4F vs 120MHz Cortex-M3, with DSP instructions and floating-point unit for complex calculations. (2) More memory - Up to 512KB Flash and 128KB SRAM vs 256KB Flash and 48KB SRAM. (3) Ethernet MAC - Integrated Ethernet with IEEE 1588 support for industrial networking. (4) Enhanced security - Hardware encryption engine for AES/DES/SHA acceleration. (5) Dual-bank Flash - Enables live firmware updates without service interruption. The MM32F5270 is recommended for applications requiring high-performance computing, digital signal processing, Ethernet connectivity, or advanced security features.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for MM32F5270 evaluation and migration guidance from MM32F3270.

faqs.question: When will MM32F5270 be available for production?
faqs.answer: The MM32F5270 series is currently in mass production and available for ordering. Initial production ramp-up began in Q1 2024, with full production capacity available now. Lead times are currently 4-6 weeks for standard packages (LQFP-64, LQFP-100). Sample quantities are available immediately from LiTong for evaluation and prototyping. Volume pricing is available for orders of 1,000 pieces or more. Contact LiTong for current pricing, lead times, and sample requests. Evaluation boards (MM32F5277-EVAL) are also available for development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for MM32F5270 samples, pricing, and production planning.

news.title: MindMotion Releases Certified BLE 5.0 Stack for MM32W Series
news.summary: MindMotion announces the release of its certified Bluetooth 5.0 protocol stack for the MM32W series wireless MCUs, enabling rapid development of IoT and smart device applications.
contentParagraphs: MindMotion Technology has released a comprehensive Bluetooth 5.0 protocol stack for its MM32W series wireless microcontrollers. The certified stack enables developers to quickly create BLE applications with full support for BLE 5.0 features including 2Mbps high-speed mode, long-range coded PHY, and extended advertising.

The BLE stack includes complete implementations of GAP, GATT, ATT, SMP, and L2CAP layers, along with standard profiles such as DIS, BAS, HRS, HTS, and HID over GATT. The stack has passed Bluetooth SIG certification, ensuring interoperability with smartphones, tablets, and other BLE devices.

Key features of the BLE stack include: Support for peripheral and central roles, multiple simultaneous connections, secure connections with LE Secure Connections, over-the-air (OTA) firmware update capability, and comprehensive power management for battery-operated devices.

The stack is provided as a library with full source code examples and documentation. Memory footprint is optimized for resource-constrained devices, requiring approximately 40KB Flash and 8KB RAM, leaving ample resources for application code.
highlights: Bluetooth SIG certified BLE 5.0 stack
Support for 2Mbps and long-range coded PHY
Complete GAP/GATT implementation with standard profiles
Secure connections and OTA firmware update support
Optimized memory footprint: 40KB Flash, 8KB RAM
news.impact: The certified BLE stack significantly reduces time-to-market for IoT device developers, eliminating the need for expensive third-party stack licenses while providing a robust, certified solution for wireless connectivity.

faqs.question: Is the BLE stack royalty-free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MindMotion BLE 5.0 stack is provided royalty-free to customers using MM32W series MCUs. The stack is included as part of the MM32 SDK at no additional cost. There are no per-unit royalties or licensing fees for products using the stack. This provides significant cost savings compared to third-party BLE stacks which often charge per-unit royalties. The stack is provided as a pre-compiled library with header files and full documentation. Source code is not provided, but comprehensive APIs and example applications enable full customization of BLE behavior.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for BLE stack licensing details and SDK access.

faqs.question: What BLE profiles are included in the stack?
faqs.answer: The MindMotion BLE 5.0 stack includes the following standard profiles: (1) GAP (Generic Access Profile) - Device discovery, connection establishment, and security. (2) GATT (Generic Attribute Profile) - Attribute protocol for data exchange. (3) DIS (Device Information Service) - Device manufacturer and model information. (4) BAS (Battery Service) - Battery level reporting. (5) HRS (Heart Rate Service) - Heart rate measurement for fitness devices. (6) HTS (Health Thermometer Service) - Temperature measurement. (7) HID over GATT - Keyboard, mouse, and custom HID devices. (8) DFU (Device Firmware Update) - Over-the-air firmware updates. Custom profiles can be implemented using the GATT API. The stack supports both peripheral and central roles, enabling flexible application architectures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for BLE profile implementation guidance and custom profile development.

news.title: MindMotion Releases Enhanced Motor Control Library with FOC Support
news.summary: MindMotion introduces an enhanced motor control software library with comprehensive FOC (Field-Oriented Control) support for MM32SPIN series MCUs, accelerating development of high-performance motor drives.
contentParagraphs: MindMotion Technology has released a comprehensive motor control library specifically designed for the MM32SPIN series motor control MCUs. The new library includes complete implementations of BLDC six-step control, PMSM field-oriented control (FOC), and AC induction motor control algorithms.

The FOC implementation leverages the hardware cordic accelerator in the MM32SPIN360, providing fast trigonometric calculations for Park/Clarke transformations and space vector PWM generation. The library supports both sensored (encoder/resolver) and sensorless (sliding mode observer, flux observer) FOC implementations.

Key features include: Complete sensorless FOC with startup algorithms, PI controller auto-tuning, MTPA (Maximum Torque Per Ampere) optimization for PMSM, field weakening for high-speed operation, and comprehensive fault protection.

The library is provided with full source code, allowing developers to customize algorithms for specific motor characteristics and application requirements. Example projects demonstrate control of BLDC, PMSM, and ACIM motors with various feedback configurations.
highlights: Complete FOC implementation with hardware cordic acceleration
Sensorless and sensored control algorithms
BLDC, PMSM, and AC induction motor support
PI auto-tuning and MTPA optimization
Full source code provided for customization
news.impact: The motor control library significantly reduces development time for motor drive applications, providing production-ready algorithms that have been tested and validated on various motor types and power levels.

faqs.question: What motor types are supported by the library?
faqs.answer: The MindMotion motor control library supports: (1) BLDC motors - Six-step trapezoidal control with sensored (Hall) and sensorless (BEMF) commutation. Sinusoidal commutation also supported. (2) PMSM motors - Field-oriented control (FOC) with both sensored (encoder) and sensorless (observer-based) position detection. (3) AC induction motors - V/Hz control and sensorless vector control. (4) Brushed DC motors - Simple PWM control (though MM32SPIN is overkill for this). The library includes specific algorithms for each motor type including startup routines, commutation logic, and protection features. For PMSM, both interior (IPMSM) and surface-mounted (SPMSM) rotor types are supported with appropriate inductance compensation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for motor control library evaluation and motor-specific algorithm selection.

faqs.question: Is the FOC library suitable for production use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MindMotion FOC library is production-ready and has been validated on various motor types and applications. The library has been tested in: (1) Appliance applications - Washing machines, refrigerators, air conditioners with PMSM compressors. (2) Power tools - Cordless drills, angle grinders with high-speed BLDC motors. (3) Industrial drives - Pumps, fans, conveyors with various power ratings. (4) Drones - High-speed propulsion motors with fast dynamic response. The library includes comprehensive fault protection and has been validated for reliability. However, as with any motor control application, proper tuning for your specific motor is required. The library provides auto-tuning functions, but manual optimization may be needed for best performance. LiTong provides technical support for library integration and tuning.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for FOC library production licensing and technical support.

news.title: MindMotion MM32F Series Achieves AEC-Q100 Automotive Certification
news.summary: Select MindMotion MM32F Series MCUs have successfully passed AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification, meeting stringent automotive reliability standards for body electronics and control applications.
contentParagraphs: MindMotion Technology announces that selected devices in the MM32F Series have achieved AEC-Q100 Grade 1 automotive qualification. The certification validates that these MCUs meet the stringent quality and reliability standards required for automotive applications.

The AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification covers operation over the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +125°C. The certification includes comprehensive testing for: high-temperature operating life (HTOL), temperature cycling, electrostatic discharge (ESD) protection, latch-up immunity, and mechanical stress robustness.

Qualified devices include the MM32F3273G7P and MM32F5277G9P in specific package options. These devices are suitable for automotive body electronics applications including: climate control modules, seat control systems, lighting control, door and window controls, and sensor interface modules.

The automotive-grade MM32F devices are manufactured on the same production lines as industrial-grade parts, with additional screening and testing to ensure automotive quality levels. PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) documentation is available for automotive customers.
highlights: AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified (-40°C to +125°C)
Comprehensive automotive reliability testing
PPAP documentation available
Suitable for body electronics applications
Same cost-effective pricing as industrial grade
news.impact: The AEC-Q100 certification enables automotive manufacturers to incorporate cost-effective MindMotion MCUs into body electronics applications, providing a domestic alternative to international suppliers while meeting automotive quality standards.

faqs.question: What is the difference between automotive and industrial grade parts?
faqs.answer: Automotive-grade (AEC-Q100) and industrial-grade MM32F devices differ in: (1) Temperature range - Automotive Grade 1: -40°C to +125°C. Industrial: -40°C to +85°C (standard) or -40°C to +105°C (extended). (2) Testing - Automotive parts undergo additional screening including 100% burn-in, tighter electrical parameter limits, and enhanced reliability testing. (3) Traceability - Full lot traceability and process control for automotive parts. (4) Documentation - PPAP documentation available for automotive parts. (5) Price - Automotive parts typically cost 10-20% more than industrial grade. (6) Availability - Not all packages and configurations are available in automotive grade. For non-automotive applications, industrial grade is usually sufficient and more cost-effective. For automotive applications requiring AEC-Q100 compliance, the automotive grade is mandatory.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for automotive grade part selection and PPAP documentation.

faqs.question: Can I use industrial grade parts in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Using industrial grade parts in automotive applications depends on the specific requirements: (1) Non-safety applications - Industrial grade may be acceptable for non-critical applications where AEC-Q100 is not strictly required. However, this is at the OEM's risk. (2) Temperature range - Ensure the industrial grade temperature range (-40°C to +85°C or +105°C) meets your application's worst-case conditions. (3) Reliability - Industrial grade has passed standard reliability testing but not the enhanced automotive screening. (4) Warranty - Using non-automotive qualified parts may affect vehicle warranty. (5) Liability - Consider product liability implications. For production automotive applications, AEC-Q100 qualified parts are strongly recommended. For development, prototyping, or non-critical aftermarket applications, industrial grade may be acceptable. Always consult with your automotive customer's quality requirements before selecting industrial grade for automotive use.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for automotive qualification guidance and grade selection recommendations.
